steps.title: Identify co-interior angles and apply the theorem
steps.content:
Since lines $\ell$ and $k$ are parallel and line $t$ is a transversal, angles $x$ and $y$ are co-interior angles (also called consecutive interior angles or same-side interior angles). These angles are on the same side of the transversal and between the parallel lines. By the co-interior angle theorem, they must be supplementary:

$$x + y = 180°$$

steps.title: Find the upper bound for y
steps.content:
From $x + y = 180°$, we can express $y = 180° - x$. Since $x > 165°$:

$$y = 180° - x < 180° - 165° = 15°$$

So $y < 15°$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
